articles.title: Telangana braces for thunderstorms as June begins; heatwave to ease gradually - Hyderabad Mail
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: Scattered thunderstorms are likely across several districts of Telangana on June 1, 2026, while heatwave conditions are expected to gradually ease from Monday, according to the day’s weather forecast. The report indicates thunderstorm activity during the evening and night hours in multiple districts, including parts of Greater Hyderabad. The copy follows newsroom principles of brevity, clarity and active voice.
Thunderstorms likely in several districts
Scattered thunderstorms are forecast in Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Medak, Rangareddy, Siddipet, Yadadri Bhuvanagiri, Nalgonda, Mahabubabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Khammam, Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Mancherial and Kamareddy districts during the evening and night.
Other parts of Telangana are expected to receive only isolated rainfall.
Hyderabad may see evening thunderstorm activity
Hyderabad is likely to remain dry until evening, after which scattered thunderstorms may occur in a few parts of the city during the evening and night.
The forecast suggests a gradual reduction in heatwave intensity across Telangana from June 1.
Temperatures to remain high in northern Telangana
Despite the expected easing of heatwave conditions, temperatures are likely to remain elevated over the next two days.
North-eastern Telangana is expected to record maximum temperatures between 41°C and 43°C, while Hyderabad may see temperatures ranging from 39°C to 41°C.

articles.title: Telangana reels under severe heatwave temperatures cross 42 degrees | Akashvani News - Newsonair
articles.full_text:
Telangana is reeling under a severe heatwave, with temperatures continuing to rise across the state. Nizamabad recorded a high of 42.4 degrees Celsius, while Adilabad and Mahabubnagar also reported temperatures above 41 degrees. Even Hyderabad crossed the 40-degree mark, with most areas witnessing temperatures 2 to 3 degrees above normal.
Amid the worsening conditions, the government has urged people to take precautions, advising to avoid stepping out between 11 AM and 3 PM. Those going outdoors have been asked to use protective gear such as hats or headscarves to prevent heat-related illnesses.
The state government has directed officials to set up drinking water stations across Gram Panchayats and mandal centres to provide relief.
The Met Department has forecast that the heatwave will persist over the next few days.
